name: Mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics bootstrap pass for June 2, 2026

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

summary: The staged baseline assigns low-confidence human_functional_benefit. Structured exercise is the direct human anchor: HIIT and combined training improve older-adult aerobic capacity while HIIT increases mitochondrial respiration, mitochondrial protein synthesis, and mitochondrial protein abundance; aerobic training increases PGC-1alpha, oxidative enzymes, MFN1, MFN2, and FIS1 in older men; resistance training improves strength and shifts a mitochondrial-function-enriched aging transcriptome toward younger levels. Druglike translation remains bounded by a posted mavodelpar PMM null functional result, metformin blunting some exercise mitochondrial adaptations, and animal-only berberine PGC-1alpha evidence.
notes: The top bootstrap queue item was selected from research/backlog/track-priority.v1.json and research/state/coverage-status.v1.json.
The public question was scoped to one mitochondrial_dysfunction track: mitochondrial-biogenesis-and-dynamics.
Structured exercise was treated as the stage anchor because it has the clearest older-adult human functional and mitochondrial-biology evidence.
Mavodelpar STRIDE was treated as a disease-specific drug boundary because posted registry results do not show a 12-minute walk advantage over placebo.
The SHAPE elamipretide registry was staged as activity/context only because it is recruiting, single-arm, and has no posted results.
The staged outlook does not imply that exercise benefits are exclusively mitochondrial or that druglike PGC-1alpha, PPAR, or cardiolipin programs have proven broad aging efficacy.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity review for the staged PubMed and ClinicalTrials.gov records.
Run interpretation/forecast review with special attention to whether structured exercise should justify human_functional_benefit for this track.
During review, check overlap boundaries with NAD/redox restoration, mitophagy enhancers, AMPK/metabolic modulators, and general exercise physiology.
If approved and published, inspect the mitochondrial dysfunction hallmark page and the mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics track page for evidence-ladder coherence.
